ns0:entry.ns0:title: [objective-c] 34306/FilzaJailedDS
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/34306/FilzaJailedDS
Language: objective-c
Filza Jailed Darksword, support iOS 17.0-26.0.1. This repo open source the tweak inject into the Filza iPA (4.0.0 and back, 4.0.2 seems crash something)

ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/haskell/ghcup-metadata
Language: haskell
GHCup metadata repository

ns0:entry.ns0:title: [go] Billionmail/BillionMail
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/Billionmail/BillionMail
Language: go
BillionMail gives you open-source MailServer, NewsLetter, Email Marketing — fully self-hosted, dev-friendly, and free from monthly fees. Join the discord: https://discord.gg/asfXzBUhZr

ns0:entry.ns0:title: [solidity] FastLane-Labs/atlas
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/FastLane-Labs/atlas
Language: solidity
An Execution Abstraction framework that allows apps, frontends, and oracles to fulfill their users' intents and retain their users' MEV by running auctions that match User Operations with Solver Operations. Users do not need a smart wallet. Permissionless for users, apps, and Solvers. Backwards-compatible with existing apps.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: [solidity] iotexproject/w3bstream
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/iotexproject/w3bstream
Language: solidity
An offchain computing layer for DePIN verifiable data computation, supporting a variety of validity proofs including Zero Knowledge (ZK), Trusted Execution Environments (TEE), and Multi-party Computation (MPC)

ns0:entry.ns0:title: [swift] FuJacob/cotabby
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/FuJacob/cotabby
Language: swift
Cotabby is local AI autocomplete for your entire Mac. Open source. On device. Everywhere you type.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: [typescript] nashsu/llm_wiki
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/nashsu/llm_wiki
Language: typescript
LLM Wiki is a cross-platform desktop application that turns your documents into an organized, interlinked knowledge base — automatically. Instead of traditional RAG (retrieve-and-answer from scratch every time), the LLM incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki from your sources。
